behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real STT model diarization quality (LLM-DEP equivalent for speech model). A stubbed transcript would hide mislabeled or dropped speaker tags (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model output property (speaker labels), not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels required by attribution mapping.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/mapping logic within a local code boundary (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor). No real OS/network/subprocess or model output quality is required. Rejected Integration: behavior text is about extraction into structure, not cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcript text fixtures with explicit assignments into the parser function/object and assert output contains normalized `{assignee, task_text}` entries.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real summarizer model judgment about coverage/completeness per attendee (LLM-DEP). Deterministic stubs would always satisfy formatting while missing true omission failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text checks one output quality criterion, not explicitly a multi-agentic handoff chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcript(s) with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization model, and verify each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic routing logic after decisions are already identified. No real infra or model output quality required. Rejected Integration: text says 'logic routes identified decisions', which disqualifies needing cross-module/infrastructure failures as the minimum boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Pass structured 'identified decision' inputs into routing logic and assert they populate `decisions` schema fields and are excluded from narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is model output quality: the summarizer must correctly surface and place decisions. Mocked outputs hide misses in salience/placement. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on final summary placement quality, not explicit validation of multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcript(s) containing explicit decisions, invoke the production summarizer, and assert those decisions appear in the `decisions` section rather than only narrative prose.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing with merge quality across segment outputs (MULTI-STEP). Real failures emerge across chained model calls and handoff/merge state, not a single call. Rejected System/Agentic as minimum: text requires end-to-end split→process sequentially→merge coherence, i.e., full pipeline sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production flow, verify segment boundaries and sequential processing order, then assert merged output is one structured summary with consistent cross-segment action items/decisions.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an explicit full agent pipeline with real infra + multiple model-dependent stages. Minimum boundary is end-to-end multi-step execution; lower levels can pass while real orchestration fails. Rejected System/Agentic: behavior text names the entire chained workflow, not one subsystem or one model call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run on a real meeting recording from upload through final delivery and assert unattended completion with valid structured output schema.
